role: Writer; poet; journalist; died in MOIS custody 1994
bio: Ali Akbar Saidi Sirjani was one of Iran's most distinguished writers, poets, and cultural commentators. Arrested on 14 March 1994 by agents of the [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence), he was announced dead eight months later of an alleged 'heart attack.' The true cause of death, later revealed by journalist Emadeddin Baghi, was a potassium suppository administered in custody. Saidi Sirjani had been a member of the [Iranian Writers' Association](/orgs/iranian-writers-association) and was an outspoken critic of censorship. His killing was one of the earlier victims of the [chain murders](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998) campaign carried out by rogue units within MOIS.
editorial_notes: Born 1931 Sirjan. Arrested 14 March 1994. Died in MOIS custody via potassium suppository. One of Iran's most prominent writers.
